workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: ~/.nakiros/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.

step.action: Load profile config from {profile_config} when available (optional).
Apply defaults when missing: communication_language={default_communication_language}, document_language={default_document_language}.

Resolve workspace scope from {workspace_config}. If the repo pointer exists, read `workspace_slug` from `{project-root}/_nakiros/workspace.yaml`, then load `~/.nakiros/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/workspace.yaml` as the canonical workspace map. If the pointer is missing, continue in single-repo mode rooted at {project-root}.
Set {{workspace_repo_list}} from canonical workspace yaml `repos`. In single-repo fallback mode, set it to the current repo only.
Set {{pm_tool}} from canonical workspace yaml `workspace.pm_tool`, defaulting to "none" when absent.
Set {{is_multi_repo}} = true when canonical workspace yaml `workspace.structure` is `multi-repo` or more than one repo is listed; otherwise false.

Load workspace-global context first by calling context.workspace.get for keys product, global, interRepo, and architecture when available. Use repo-local `_nakiros/` slices only for repo-owned detail.
step.output: [QA] Étape 1/6 — Load configuration ✓ Config loaded | PM: {{pm_tool or "standalone"}} | Workspace: {{is_multi_repo ? "multi-repo" : "single-repo"}}

step.ask: What are you reviewing? (ticket ID, MR/PR URL, or describe the change)
step.action: Capture as {{review_target}}.

check.if: review_target looks like a ticket ID and pm_tool is configured
check.action: Fetch ticket details via the configured Nakiros PM integration: title, description, acceptance criteria, status, labels.
Capture as {{ticket_context}}. Extract acceptance criteria as {{acceptance_criteria}}.

check.if: review_target looks like a MR/PR URL
check.action: Attempt to fetch MR/PR diff using available CLI (gh pr diff / gitlab mr diff).

check.if: no PM integration and no CLI available
check.ask: Paste the acceptance criteria or describe what this change is supposed to do:

step.output: [QA] Étape 2/6 — Identify review target ✓ Review target identified: {{review_target}}

step.critical: This is a BLOCKING gate. If criteria are ambiguous, raise the issue before proceeding.
step.action: Analyze {{acceptance_criteria}} against these testability criteria: - Each criterion is verifiable (pass / fail — no subjective judgment) - Each criterion has a clear scope (which component, which user action, which state) - Edge cases and error states are covered (not just the happy path) - Non-functional requirements are explicit if relevant (performance, accessibility, security)

check.if: any criterion is ambiguous or missing
check.action: List each ambiguous criterion with a concrete explanation of what is missing.
Propose explicit, testable rewrites for each ambiguous criterion.
check.output: [QA] ⚠ AC gap detected — review cannot proceed until criteria are confirmed:
check.ask: Please clarify or confirm the revised criteria:

check.if: all criteria are clear and testable

step.output: [QA] Étape 3/6 — AC validation ✓ AC validated — {{confirmed_acceptance_criteria | count}} criteria confirmed

step.action: Scan for existing test files relative to the change scope ({{changed_files}} or inferred from {{review_target}}):
Look for: *.test.ts, *.spec.ts, *.test.tsx, *.spec.tsx, *_test.py, *Test.java, *Spec.rb, etc.
For each confirmed acceptance criterion, determine if a corresponding test exists:
Classify each gap as: critical (blocks sign-off), moderate (should be fixed before merge), minor (follow-up).

check.action: Extend scan to integration test directories across repos in {{workspace_repo_list}}.
Identify cross-repo integration points covered by the change.

step.action: Build coverage matrix: | Criterion | Test exists? | Test file | Gap severity | |---|---|---|---| | ... | Yes / No / Partial | path or — | — / critical / moderate / minor |
step.output: [QA] Étape 4/6 — Test coverage analysis ✓ Coverage analysis complete

step.action: Identify the regression risk zone from the change set:

For each changed file/module: - List existing tests that exercise this file - Identify callers / dependents that may be affected (static analysis if feasible) - Flag integration points with other repos (if multi-repo)
Produce a regression run list: which test commands to run, in which order.
step.output: [QA] Étape 5/6 — Regression scope ✓ Regression scope defined

step.goal: Write QA review artifact and sign-off decision
step.action: Ensure directory exists: {repo_review_dir}.
Generate artifact filename: {{qa_file}} = {repo_review_dir}/qa-review-{{date}}-{{review_target | slugify}}.md
